title: Stakeholder Task, Workflow Fit, Authority, Risk, and Evidence · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent organization supplies a different user population, task distribution, tool set, risk tier, model response, resource budget, accessibility need, and failure condition and transfers stakeholder task, workflow fit, authority, risk, and evidence into a changed bounded agent product and risk charter. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded agent product and risk charter with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, expl…
objectives: Define stakeholder, user population, decision, task distribution, observable value, failure, review cadence, and non-goals before choosing a model.
Compare deterministic code, workflow, model-assisted step, and agent loop and choose the least variable architecture that satisfies the task.
Classify impact, reversibility, affected groups, uncertainty, data sensitivity, action authority, and required human oversight.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Stakeholder Task
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab agent engineering team handles case 7577 during a controlled support-operations rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate agent construction on stakeholder value, workflow fit, authority, measurable failure, risk controls, and escalation; every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy; then change one tool call, argument, authority, result, or failure and preserve correlation. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-nongoals-escalation-pk7yk7" that uses case 8906 to gate agent construction on stakeholder value, workflow fit, authority, measurable failure, risk controls, and escalation. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-nongoals-escalation-pk7yk7
# Competency: agentpy-nongoals-escalation.
# Case 8906: a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review.
# Operating constraint: model output and tool arguments remain untrusted until validated.
# Changed case: change model capability, API version, schema, or provider state and revalidate the adapter.
def evidence_agentpy_nongoals_escalation_pk7yk7(authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 8, risk_controls: int = 6, unresolved_high_risks: int = 0, escalation_paths: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_pk7yk7 = "pk7yk7-7ky7kp-8906"
    assert evidence_variant_pk7yk7
    evidence_axes_pk7yk7 = (
        "authoritypk7yk7",
        "interaction8906",
        "tool7ky7kp",
        "statepk7yk78906",
        "evaluation8906pk7yk7",
        "recovery7ky7kp8906",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_pk7yk7)) == 6
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "authority-missing"
    if acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "outcome-underdefined"
    if risk_controls < 4 or unresolved_high_risks > 0:
        return False, risk_controls, "risk-uncontrolled"
    if escalation_paths < 2:
        return False, escalation_paths, "escalation-missing"
    assert authorized and acceptance_cases >= 4 and unresolved_high_risks == 0
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-agent-charter"
